reasoning: The initial BLS data shows a drop of 97,000 federal employees from the January 2025 peak through August 2025. Recent months (July/August) only saw reductions of 12,000-15,000 jobs per month. In May 2025, a court reportedly barred most major agencies from issuing mass layoffs indefinitely, though other tactics like early retirement or relocation might encourage attrition. Projecting the 15k/month pace forward for the last four months of the year yields roughly an additional 60k lost jobs, bringing the total to ~157k, well short of the 250k required. However, the exact figures for September and October 2025 are unknown in this text search, and the Kalshi market reached 46% at the end of October. The market may be anticipating an acceleration in Q4 or a large year-end buy-out/firing push by the Trump/DOGE administration. I anchor near the Kalshi price but adjust slightly downwards given the current visible data trajectory and legal barriers to mass layoffs.
